texts.full_text: skip nodal (ISN) disease in the abdomen. A 58-year-old male presented for evaluation due to an incidental finding on chest imaging during workup unrelated pulmonary symptoms, revealing multiple bilateral cavitary lesions suggestive of malignancy. Subsequent CT-guided biopsy confirmed primary adenocarcinoma histology with no evidence of distant metastases at initial staging other than a suspicious enlarged abdominal lymph node. Further investigation through PET-CT demonstrated hypermetabolic activity in the left lower lobe and an isolated skip nodal involvement within periaortic nodes, without any mediastinal or hilar engagement. This pattern was highly unusual as typical progression involves contiguous spread to regional structures before dista

texts.full_text: h him. In the first week of her marriage, her then-husband showed signs of abusive behavior. Allegedly, her husband repeatedly threatened to kill her and was violent. Reiss only realized that she was experiencing domestic violence when she spoke with a therapist outside of her community. Allegedly, her community members encouraged her to stay with her husband. After a particular episode of violence, Reiss went to get a temporary restraining order, and was the first woman in her Orthodox Jewish community to do so. However, her rabbi sent an attorney (who was also from the Orthodox Jewish community) to take Fraidy to family court to tell the judge that she wished to drop the order. Reiss was able to go to college and get a job, and thus she felt that she was able to support her family and leave her husband and get a divorce. She developed a five-year escape plan. Reiss graduated at 32 from Rutgers University, anand she later went on to earn a Master's degree from the University of Pennsylvania. She used her education and experiences to found the non-profit organization, Unchained at Last, to help other women and girls escape from and prevent forced and child marriages. Unchained at Last, which was established in 2002, has since helped thousands of wome

texts.preview_head: abstract: 'Optical microtraps provide a strong spatial confinement for laser-cooled atoms. They can, e.g., be realized with strongly focused trapping light beams or the optical near fields of nano-sca
texts.preview_tail: nable environments.' Keywords: optical microtraps, Bose-Einstein condensate, nanofiber-based optical dipole trap, atom manipulation, state-dependent trapping, quantum gas control, quantum simulations,
texts.full_text: abstract: 'Optical microtraps provide a strong spatial confinement for laser-cooled atoms. They can, e.g., be realized with strongly focused trapping light beams or the optical near fields of nano-scale waveguides and photonic nanostructures. Atoms in such traps often experience strongly spatially varying AC Stark shifts which are proportional to the magnetic quantum number of the respective energy level. These inhomogeneous fictitious magnetic fields can cause a displacement of the trapping potential that depends on the Zeeman state. Hitherto, this effect was mainly perceived as detrimental. However, it also provides a means to probe and to manipulate the motional state of the atoms in the trap by driving transitions between Zeeman states. Furthermore, by applying additional real or fictitious magnetic fields, the state-dependence of the trapping potential can be controlled. Here, using laser-cooled atoms that are confined in a nanofiber-based optical dipole trap, we employ this control in order to tune the internal state of the atoms and to manipulate the spatial and motional state of the trapped quantum gas. We demonstrate the potential of this method by observing the formation of a Bose-Einstein condensate in a state- and spatially-controlled manner. This opens new possibilities for quantum simulations and for the study of quantum many-body phenomena in controlled and tunable environments.' Keywords: optical microtraps, Bose-Einstein condensate, nanofiber-based optical dipole trap, atom manipulation, state-dependent trapping, quantum gas control, quantum simulations,
